name: Coconut meat, raw (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh coconut kernel (mature endosperm)

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 170169 (SR Legacy, Nuts, coconut meat, raw)
components.evidence: USDA moisture ~46.99 g; Kenyan varietal study 43.2-48.2% (Adoyo 2021, DOI:10.5897/AJFS2021.2116)

components.name: total lipid (fat)
components.evidence: Dominant energy source; ~91% saturated, medium-chain. Kenyan study 35.0-38.3% crude fat

components.name: total carbohydrate
components.evidence: Mostly cell-wall/storage polysaccharide (mannan/galactomannan) + fiber; free sugars only ~6 g

components.name: mannan / galactomannan (cell-wall & storage polysaccharide)
components.source: Senphan & Benjakul / IntechOpen coconut chapter (2024) chapters/1179190
components.evidence: Mature kernel reserve carbohydrate is galactomannan: ~61% polysaccharide of which 26% mannans, 13% cellulose; the bulk of the 15 g carbohydrate + fiber. Major fermentable non-sugar carbon after lipid

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: Free soluble sugars; low and decreasing with maturity

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 170169 (total sugars) + PMC7174220 / coconut sap-sugar literature
components.evidence: Predominant free sugar in mature kernel (sucrose rises with maturity while glucose/fructose fall); individual split not independently quantified for solid kernel, but sucrose is the dominant fraction of the 6.23 g total

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Minor free monosaccharide, decreases as kernel matures

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Minor free monosaccharide, lowest of the three, decreases with maturity

components.name: myo-inositol
components.evidence: Present in coconut; abundant in coconut water, minor in meat

components.name: lauric acid (C12:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 170169; Adoyo 2021 (45.9-50.7% of total FA)
components.evidence: DOMINANT fatty acid and the single largest carbon/energy source in coconut meat

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid in coconut meat

components.name: malic acid
components.source: coconut compositional literature (present, unquantified in mature meat)
components.evidence: Organic acids are MINOR in mature coconut meat and concentrate in coconut water (malic > citric > succinic/oxalic there). Included for completeness; not a dominant carbon source in the solid kernel

components.name: citric acid
components.source: coconut compositional literature (present, unquantified in mature meat)

components.name: shikimic acid
components.evidence: Minor organic acid reported in coconut tissues

remove.name: Arsenate

remove.reason: lanthanide, non-nutritive trace contaminant / FooDB noise

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: ultra-trace non-nutritive element, no established role

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: ultra-trace element, not a defined growth nutrient here / FooDB noise

remove.name: Bromine
remove.reason: trace halogen contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Fluorine
remove.reason: trace halogen, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Calcium oxide
remove.reason: not a real food chemical species; ashing artifact for reporting Ca, already covered by calcium

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial-fermentation food preservative additive, not a native coconut nutrient; FooDB cross-listing

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin (Apiaceae/citrus phytochemical), biologically implausible in coconut; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Isoflavones
remove.reason: legume (soy) phytochemical class, implausible in coconut; FooDB predicted/cross-listing

remove.name: Genistein
remove.reason: soy isoflavone, implausible in coconut; FooDB predicted/cross-listing

remove.name: (+/-)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: non-physiological racemate artifact; real L-isoleucine already listed

remove.name: Methoxyl
remove.reason: chemical fragment/substituent, not a discrete nutrient compound

remove.name: Cobalt (Co2+)
remove.reason: ultra-trace, not an established coconut-meat nutrient (no B12 in a plant food); FooDB noise

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: ultra-trace, not an established growth nutrient here; FooDB noise

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: ultra-trace, unquantified, likely FooDB noise/contaminant

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: ultra-trace, unquantified; retain only if model requires Mo cofactor

remove.name: Iodine
remove.reason: ultra-trace, not an established coconut-meat nutrient; FooDB noise

missing_added: quantified all fatty acids C6:0-C18:2 (lauric 14.86 g dominant)
quantified full amino-acid panel (arginine 0.55 g, glutamate 0.76 g highest)
quantified full vitamin panel (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B9/C/E/K) + choline 12.1 mg
quantified full mineral panel (K 356, P 113, Mg 32, Ca 14, Fe 2.43, Mn 1.5, Zn 1.1, Cu 0.44 mg, Se 10.1 ug)
dietary fiber 9.0 g and mannan/galactomannan storage polysaccharide flagged as the major non-lipid carbon reserve
organic acids (malic/citric/shikimic) noted as minor in mature meat (concentrate in coconut water)
keep_uncovered: gamma-tocopherol (genuine vitamer, no distinct BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170169 (SR Legacy: Nuts, coconut meat, raw) - proximates, sugars, fatty acids, amino acids, minerals, vitamins
nutritionvalue.org / fitaudit (USDA 170169 mirrors) - per-nutrient fatty acid & amino acid amounts, choline
Wikipedia Coconut USDA nutrition table (FDC 170169) - vitamins & minerals
Adoyo, Sila & Onyango 2021, Afr. J. Food Sci. 15(8):313-321, DOI:10.5897/AJFS2021.2116 - Kenyan varietal proximates, lauric dominance
PMC12691760 (Foods 2025) - fatty acid & phenolic profiles of mature vs tender coconut
IntechOpen coconut chapter chapters/1179190 - mature-kernel galactomannan/mannan reserve carbohydrate
PMC7174220 / coconut sap & maturation sugar literature - sucrose-dominant free sugar with maturity
FooDB FOOD00336 - broad compound listing (source of curated components and removed noise)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe on raw coconut meat are (1) medium-chain-fatty-acid triacylglycerols, above all lauric acid (14.9 g/100g), and (2) the mannan/galactomannan cell-wall storage polysaccharide plus fiber (~24 g combined); free fermentable sugars are low (~6.2 g total, predominantly sucrose) and organic acids are minor (they concentrate in coconut water, not the solid kernel). Removed 4 toxic heavy metals, ~20 non-nutritive trace/lanthanide elements, and several implausible/predicted FooDB compounds (oxypeucedanin, soy isoflavones/genistein, epsilon-polylysine).
